management_mcp_server.pin_ssot: TWO decoupled SSOT values, so a version bump NEVER fail-closes a not-yet-rebuilt image (the npm/pypi semver-range model). (1) `compatible_range` is what the LAUNCH resolves: the plugin fragment + boot launch use `@molecule-ai/mcp-server@<compatible_range>`, and `npx --prefer-offline` resolves it to the HIGHEST in-range version already baked in THAT image's npm cache, ENTIRELY OFFLINE, whatever concrete version that image happens to hold. (2) `pinned_version` is the ONE concrete in-range version the CURRENT release's prebake bakes (a deterministic build target). Guard D is now DRIFT-TOLERANT: baked == pinned_version (this release bakes exactly this) AND pinned_version satisfies compatible_range AND the fragment launch arg == compatible_range. Bumping pinned_version + re-baking is therefore a ROLLING update — an older image that baked an EARLIER in-range version keeps launching fine because its baked version still satisfies compatible_range, so there is NO fleet-wide fail-close window (local/staging/prod each launch whatever they baked). Only a BREAKING (major) change moves compatible_range (e.g. `^2.0.0`) and warrants a coordinated rebuild.

prebake.owner: base runtime (molecule_runtime prebake helper) — the default EVERY runtime image inherits; a template adapter overrides ONLY for a divergent node location, and usually should not need to (ADR-004: SDK contract -> base-runtime default -> per-adapter override-if-needed).
prebake.rationale: The kind=platform concierge launches the mgmt-MCP via `npx --prefer-offline @molecule-ai/mcp-server@<pinned_version>` inside the runtime boot enumeration spawn (a HARD deadline). The runtime image MUST pre-bake this EXACT version into the agent npm cache (/home/agent/.npm) at build time so the launch resolves ENTIRELY OFFLINE. A missing/stale bake -> cold pull -> ETARGET / CF-WAF throttle -> #1027 'management MCP FAILED TO LAUNCH' -> fail-close (the launch-side of RCA #2970; the fetch-side is the git-native plugin source).
prebake.conformance: In the built image, BOTH `npx -y --offline @molecule-ai/mcp-server@<pinned_version>` (the exact version this release baked) AND `npx -y --offline @molecule-ai/mcp-server@<compatible_range>` (the RANGE the launch actually uses — it MUST resolve OFFLINE to the baked version) with MOLECULE_MCP_MODE=management MUST return a tools/list containing `required_tool` (provision_workspace) with ZERO network, AND MUST resolve under a FOREIGN HOME (not only the build HOME) — see home_independent. The range check is the one that mirrors the real boot launch; the build FAILS otherwise.
prebake.home_independent: The scoped-registry + npm cache config MUST be applied GLOBALLY (node-prefix etc/npmrc via `npm config set --global`), NOT a per-user ${HOME}/.npmrc. The runtime spawns the mgmt-MCP under a launch HOME that is NOT guaranteed to be the agent home (observed HOME=/root in the local docker provisioner), so a HOME-local config is MISSED and npx falls through to the public registry -> ETARGET on the private @molecule-ai scope -> #1027 fail-close even though the version is correctly baked. The prebake self-check MUST therefore verify offline resolution under a FOREIGN HOME so this can never regress.
prebake.lockstep: Guard D (drift-TOLERANT, semver-range): (a) image-baked version == pinned_version (this release bakes exactly one concrete version); (b) pinned_version satisfies compatible_range; (c) the plugin fragment launch arg == compatible_range (the RANGE, NOT the exact version) so every image launches whatever in-range version it baked. Enforced ONCE centrally (SDK conformance / base runtime), never duplicated per template. This REPLACES the old exact-equality lockstep, whose bump-ahead-of-rebuild created a fleet-wide ETARGET fail-close window.

descriptor: runtime-agnostic name->{command,args?,env?}; the plugin is the SSOT for this descriptor. The MCP-wiring PORT (InstallContext.register_mcp_server -> BaseAdapter.register_mcp_server_hook) renders the descriptor into the native config the ACTIVE runtime reads. The per-runtime `runtimes` map pins each native surface; no runtime reads the others' files.

port.dispatch: ADR-004: the BaseAdapter default hook is NAME-AGNOSTIC (writes the generic JSON mcpServers map via mcp_render.render_json_mcp_servers on the default settings path; management_mcp_present via mcp_render.json_mcp_servers_has) — no self.name() dispatch, no engine per-runtime table. Each official adapter (claude-code/codex/hermes/openclaw) OVERRIDES register_mcp_server_hook + management_mcp_present to render/read its OWN native file; a not-yet-migrated adapter uses the base JSON default.
port.resolver_default: plugins_registry.resolve defaults an mcpServers-shaped plugin to MCPServerAdaptor (AdaptorSource.MCP_SERVER) for ANY runtime, so molecule-platform-mcp needs no per-runtime adapters/<runtime>.py.

self.$comment: The `self` credential is delivered as a re-read FILE PATH, NOT as an env VALUE: the runtime sets token_file_env (MOLECULE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_FILE) to the path credential_file (/configs/.auth_token), and the mcp-server re-reads that file per call. Injecting the MOLECULE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN env VALUE instead would 401 after restart-rotation — /configs/.auth_token is rotated on restart, so a token value snapshotted into the child env goes stale (RFC review BLOCKER).
